Pneumonia Detection AI v2.1

This system analyzes chest X-ray images to detect pneumonia and classify severity.
It accepts DICOM images as input and outputs a binary prediction (pneumonia/no pneumonia)
along with a confidence score between 0 and 1.

The system is deployed in emergency radiology departments at three hospital sites.
It processes approximately 200 X-rays per day per site and is integrated into the
radiology information system (RIS). Predictions are delivered to the requesting
physician within 2 minutes of image upload.

The model was trained on 50,000 chest X-rays from a single academic medical center.
It uses a ResNet-50 architecture fine-tuned on the pneumonia classification task.
Model updates are deployed via a rolling release process without downtime.

Input: DICOM chest X-ray image
Output: {prediction: "PNEUMONIA" | "NORMAL", confidence: 0.0–1.0}
Deployment context: Emergency radiology, time-sensitive clinical decisions
